methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terra Drone is a publicly listed company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, founded by Toru Tokushige and traded on the Tokyo exchange. The valuation universe for direct comparison is constrained: while five private peers operate in the sector—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and ACSL—no public comparables exist in the same domain. This absence of public peers limits traditional trading multiples analysis.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable to this valuation. The narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous contains insufficient datapoints when filtered by stage, development proximity, or valuation tier; the methodology requires a minimum of three same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables to generate reliable median benchmarks, and none of the available screens yielded that threshold. Attempting to derive multiples from the full five-company private set would introduce material distortion due to heterogeneous development stages and business models across the group.

The analysis therefore cannot produce a defensible valuation range using standard VC triangulation methods. Resolution would require either: expanded data on the five private peers' recent financials and valuation events; identification of adjacent-sector public companies with sufficient operational similarity; or alternative methodologies such as discounted cash flow analysis using Terra Drone-specific projections. The absence of public comparables and narrow private peer set represents the primary valuation constraint.
